brand.name: Louis Vuitton
brand.description: French luxury fashion and leather goods house founded by Louis Vuitton in Paris in 1854, originally as a malletier (trunk maker). Now the flagship maison of the LVMH group and one of the most counterfeited luxury brands in the world, making its bags a high-priority authentication target.

style.name: Neverfull
style.closure_type: open top with side drawstring cinch and interior hook clasp
style.description: The Neverfull is Louis Vuitton's signature unstructured shopper tote, introduced in 2007. It features side laces/drawstrings that cinch to adjust width, a roomy open top with an interior hook closure, and is offered in three sizes (PM, MM, GM) and the house's coated-canvas lines (Monogram, Damier Ebene, Damier Azur) as well as leather and seasonal versions. A 2013 update ('New Model'/NM) introduced a detachable zipped pochette and expanded interior lining colors.
style.notes: Year introduced 2007 and the 2013 update (detachable pochette + expanded linings, sometimes called the 'NM' New Model) are corroborated across multiple snippet sources (l'Etoile, Sotheby's, Rebag, myGemma). All values snippet-sourced; verify against rendered Louis Vuitton heritage pages before upgrading confidence.

variants.exterior_colorway: Monogram brown coated canvas with natural cowhide (Vachetta) leather trim and handles
variants.hardware_type: side drawstring cinch with leather laces; interior hook clasp; D-ring
variants.interior_material: textile lining (current); striped textile on 2007-2013 era
variants.stitching_color: tonal yellow/tan saddle stitching on Vachetta
variants.authentication_markers: Smallest of the three sizes. Approximate current dimensions L29 x H22 x W13 cm (sources also cite ~29 x 21 x 12). Unstructured Monogram coated canvas with Vachetta (untreated cowhide) handles and trim that patinas with age. Side leather drawstrings cinch the silhouette. Current versions ship with a removable zipped pochette. Heat stamp 'LOUIS VUITTON / made in [country]' should be thin, crisp, level, with round O's. Snippet-sourced; needs browser/physical verification.
variants.notes: Dimensions vary slightly across resale snippet sources (29x22x13 vs 29x21x12); recorded in production_records with a note. Retail price left null: I found a well-corroborated 2024/2025 figure for the MM (~$2,030) but not a separately corroborated PM figure, so I did not guess it.

variants.exterior_colorway: Monogram brown coated canvas with natural cowhide (Vachetta) leather trim and handles
variants.hardware_type: side drawstring cinch with leather laces; interior hook clasp; D-ring
variants.interior_material: textile lining (current); striped textile on 2007-2013 era
variants.stitching_color: tonal yellow/tan saddle stitching on Vachetta
variants.authentication_markers: The most popular size. Approximate current dimensions L31 x H28 x W14 cm (resale sources also cite L32 x H29 x W17 for some eras). Unstructured Monogram coated canvas with Vachetta handles/trim. Side leather drawstrings cinch the silhouette. Current versions ship with a removable zipped pochette. Heat stamp should be thin, crisp, level, evenly spaced, with round O's; side tab stitching symmetrical and uniform. Snippet-sourced; needs verification.
variants.notes: Retail price $2,030 USD reflects multiple 2024/2025 snippet sources (CloverSac, eBay listing 'Retail Price $2030', Bagaholic price list) for the Monogram MM; treat as approximate as LV raises prices frequently and figure was not confirmed on the live LV site. Dimensions vary by source/era; see production_records.

variants.hardware_type: side drawstring cinch with leather laces; interior hook clasp; D-ring

Side leather drawstrings cinch the silhouette. Current versions ship with a removable zipped pochette. Same heat-stamp and stitching authentication checks as other sizes. Snippet-sourced; needs verification.
variants.notes: GM dimensions (L39 x H32 x W19 cm) are internally consistent across several resale size-guide snippets. Retail price left null (not separately corroborated).

variants.exterior_colorway: Damier Ebene dark brown checkerboard coated canvas with smooth leather trim and handles
variants.hardware_type: side drawstring cinch with leather laces; interior hook clasp; D-ring
variants.authentication_markers: Damier Ebene checkerboard coated canvas. Unlike the Monogram line, Damier Ebene uses smooth darker leather trim (not untreated Vachetta) and was historically lined in red. Current LV product code commonly cited as N40599 for the MM. Same drawstring, pochette and heat-stamp authentication checks. Snippet-sourced; needs verification.
variants.notes: Product code N40599 appeared in a louisvuitton.com result snippet for the Damier Ebene MM; treat as indicative, not verified. Red lining for Damier Ebene is widely repeated across sources. year_start 2008 approximate (Damier Ebene Neverfull followed the 2007 Monogram launch); flagged low certainty on exact year.

variants.exterior_colorway: Damier Azur light blue/cream checkerboard coated canvas with natural cowhide (Vachetta) leather trim and handles
variants.hardware_type: side drawstring cinch with leather laces; interior hook clasp; D-ring
variants.interior_material: textile lining (beige on early era; expanded colors current)
variants.stitching_color: tonal yellow/tan saddle stitching on Vachetta
variants.authentication_markers: Damier Azur light checkerboard coated canvas with Vachetta handles/trim (like Monogram, the untreated leather patinas). Early-era Azur was lined in beige textile. Same drawstring, pochette and heat-stamp authentication checks. Snippet-sourced; needs verification.
variants.notes: Damier Azur Neverfull is well attested in resale guides as one of the canonical lines; exact dimensions assumed equal to the Monogram MM but not separately confirmed, so production dimensions left null. Retail price null.

production_records.date_code_format: ERA-DEPENDENT. Bags produced roughly 2007-March 2021 carry a stamped, human-readable date code: two letters (factory/country location code) followed by four digits, where digits 1 and 3 = the week of the year and digits 2 and 4 = the year (e.g. a code ending pattern decoding to week/year). Around March 2021 LV DISCONTINUED the stamped date code and replaced it with an embedded RFID microchip; newer bags have NO human-readable date code at all. Do NOT treat a date code as universal, and do NOT rely on it as sole proof of authenticity. Do not trust any factory-location-code lookup table without verification.
production_records.stamp_placement: Date-code era: stamped discreetly inside the bag (commonly on an interior seam tab or near the interior pocket). RFID era: chip sewn into the lining, readable only by Louis Vuitton boutique equipment.
production_records.stamp_font_notes: Heat stamp font ('LOUIS VUITTON / made in [country]') should be thin, crisp, perfectly level and evenly spaced with nearly round O's; bold, blurry, crooked or unevenly pressed text is a red flag. Snippet-sourced.
production_records.known_authentication_markers: Vachetta (untreated cowhide) handles/trim that patina over time; symmetrical, uniform side-tab stitching with warm-yellow thread; commonly cited ~2 cm spacing from the top of the bag to the heat-stamp tab / hardware stitching (anything notably off is a warning sign). Dimensions vary slightly across sources (L29 x H22 x W13 vs L29 x H21 x W12). All snippet-sourced; verify physically.

production_records.date_code_format: Same era-dependent system as all Neverfulls: stamped two-letter + four-digit date code on bags ~2007-March 2021 (digits 1/3 = week, 2/4 = year); RFID microchip and NO readable date code after ~March 2021. Not a standalone authenticity test.
production_records.stamp_placement: Date-code era: interior seam tab / near interior pocket. RFID era: chip embedded in lining.
production_records.stamp_font_notes: Heat stamp thin, crisp, level, evenly spaced, round O's; 'L' has a short tail, the O looks larger than other letters, and the strokes of the TT nearly touch (widely repeated authentication detail; snippet-sourced).
production_records.known_authentication_markers: Vachetta handles/trim; uniform symmetric side-tab stitching in warm yellow thread; ~2 cm spacing cited from bag top to heat-stamp/hardware stitching. Dimensions vary by source/era: L31 x H28 x W14 cm is the most commonly cited current figure; some resale sources cite ~L32 x H29 x W17. Snippet-sourced.

production_records.date_code_format: Same era-dependent system: stamped date code ~2007-March 2021; RFID microchip with no readable code after ~March 2021. Not a standalone authenticity test.
production_records.stamp_placement: Date-code era: interior seam tab / near interior pocket. RFID era: chip embedded in lining.
production_records.stamp_font_notes: Heat stamp thin, crisp, level, evenly spaced with round O's; snippet-sourced.
production_records.known_authentication_markers: Vachetta handles/trim; uniform symmetric side-tab stitching. Dimensions L39 x H32 x W19 cm internally consistent across size-guide snippets. Snippet-sourced.

production_records.date_code_format: Same era-dependent system: stamped date code ~2007-March 2021; RFID microchip with no readable code after ~March 2021.
production_records.stamp_placement: Date-code era: interior seam tab / near interior pocket. RFID era: chip embedded in lining.
production_records.stamp_font_notes: Heat stamp thin, crisp, level, round O's; snippet-sourced.
production_records.known_authentication_markers: Damier Ebene coated canvas with smooth darker leather trim (not Vachetta) and historically a red microfiber lining. MM dimensions assumed equal to the Monogram MM (L31 x H28 x W14 cm); not separately confirmed for Damier Ebene. Snippet-sourced.

production_records.date_code_format: Same era-dependent system: stamped date code ~2007-March 2021; RFID microchip with no readable code after ~March 2021.
production_records.stamp_placement: Date-code era: interior seam tab / near interior pocket. RFID era: chip embedded in lining.
production_records.stamp_font_notes: Heat stamp thin, crisp, level, round O's; snippet-sourced.
production_records.known_authentication_markers: Damier Azur light coated canvas with Vachetta handles/trim. Dimensions presumed equal to Monogram MM but not separately confirmed, so left null. Snippet-sourced.

known_color_combinations.authentication_notes: From 2007 to ~2014 the original-model Monogram Neverfull was made only with beige striped textile lining (cursive lettering / floral-stripe print). Source: l'Etoile, Sotheby's, Yoogi's Closet (snippet-level).

known_color_combinations.interior_color: expanded textile colors (e.g. Rose Ballerine, Fuchsia, Mimosa, Cerise/Cherry)
known_color_combinations.authentication_notes: The 2013 'New Model' (NM) update introduced an updated lining print (plain block lettering and plain stripes vs the earlier cursive/floral) plus expanded interior color options and the detachable zipped pochette. Source: l'Etoile, Rebag, myGemma (snippet-level).
Current LV catalog offers a Monogram MM with cherry red lining (product references M46975 / M46987 in LV site snippets). Snippet-level.

known_color_combinations.exterior_color: Damier Ebene dark brown checkerboard coated canvas
known_color_combinations.authentication_notes: Damier Ebene Neverfull historically lined in red. Smooth darker leather trim rather than Vachetta. Source: Yoogi's Closet, PurseForum (snippet-level).

known_color_combinations.exterior_color: Damier Azur light blue/cream checkerboard coated canvas
known_color_combinations.authentication_notes: Early Damier Azur, like Monogram, was lined in beige before the lining-color expansion. Vachetta handles/trim. Source: l'Etoile, PurseForum (snippet-level).

carry_methods.notes: Primary carry: dual fixed flat top handles designed to be worn over the shoulder or carried by hand. Exact handle drop not confirmed from an accessible source, so strap_drop_length_cm left null. Snippet-sourced.
The flat top handles also allow hand / crook-of-arm carry. The Neverfull has no crossbody strap in its classic configuration.

fits.notes: Owner reviews widely describe the MM as fitting a small laptop/tablet plus daily essentials; treat as approximate, not confirmed for a specific model. Snippet-sourced.
The MM's roomy unstructured body accommodates a water bottle per owner reviews. Snippet-sourced, low certainty on specific bottle size.
The smaller PM is generally described as an everyday/essentials tote; a laptop is a tight or no fit depending on size. Snippet-sourced, low certainty.
The GM is frequently cited as a travel/work tote that holds a larger laptop and more. Snippet-sourced.

interior_storage.authentication_notes: Single interior flat/open pocket is standard on the Neverfull. Snippet-sourced.
Interior leather tab with a D-ring (commonly used to clip the included pochette or keys). Snippet-sourced.

interior_storage.placement: removable zipped pochette (detachable, can also clip to interior D-ring)
interior_storage.material: coated canvas / leather, matching the bag line
interior_storage.authentication_notes: Current Neverfulls (since the 2013 update) ship with a removable zipped pochette/clutch that doubles as an extra interior pocket. Earlier 2007-2013 bags did not include it. Snippet-sourced.

serial_tags.format: Two letters (factory/country location code) followed by four digits. For bags from ~2007 to March 2021, digits 1 and 3 indicate the week of the year and digits 2 and 4 indicate the year. (Earlier pre-2007 LV formats differ and are out of scope for the Neverfull, which launched 2007.)
serial_tags.placement: Stamped discreetly inside the bag, commonly on an interior seam/leather tab or near the interior pocket; placement varies by model and era.
serial_tags.material: Heat-stamped into leather tab or stamped on lining-adjacent tab.
serial_tags.how_to_read: First two letters = factory/country location code (do NOT rely on unverified location-code lookup tables). Remaining four digits: positions 1 and 3 together = week of the year; positions 2 and 4 together = year. The decoded week/year should be plausible for the canvas, hardware and lining style. A date code is NOT sole proof of authenticity and is easily faked.
serial_tags.authentication_notes: Date code is era-specific and was DISCONTINUED around March 2021. Do not present it as universal. Snippet-sourced; verify physically.

serial_tags.format: Embedded RFID microchip with no human-readable number; readable only by Louis Vuitton boutique equipment.
serial_tags.placement: Sewn into the lining of the bag.
serial_tags.how_to_read: Not human-readable. Newer Neverfulls have NO visible/stamped date code. A seller claiming a stamped date code 'proves' a brand-new 2022+ bag is authentic should be treated with caution, because genuine recent bags do not have one.
serial_tags.authentication_notes: Critical era distinction: around March 2021 LV replaced the stamped date code with this embedded RFID chip. Absence of a date code on a recent bag is normal, not a red flag. Snippet-sourced; verify.

locks_and_keys.authentication_notes: The Neverfull is an open-top tote that closes via side drawstrings and a single interior hook clasp; it does not ship with a padlock-and-key set or clochette (unlike LV's Speedy/Keepall lines). A padlock/clochette on a classic Neverfull would be inconsistent with the style. Snippet-sourced.

provenance_packaging.description: Louis Vuitton dust bag included with new purchase. Exact current design/branding not independently confirmed for the Neverfull specifically.
provenance_packaging.material: Fabric (cotton/flannel-style consistent with LV packaging practice)
provenance_packaging.branding: Louis Vuitton wordmark/logo; exact current design not confirmed
provenance_packaging.authentication_notes: Presence of a dust bag does not by itself authenticate a bag. Snippet-sourced; design details not verified this pass.

provenance_packaging.description: Removable zipped pochette/pouch included with current (post-2013) Neverfulls; doubles as a clutch or extra interior pocket and clips to the interior D-ring. Frequently sold/lost separately on the resale market, so a complete set should include it.
provenance_packaging.material: Coated canvas / leather matching the bag line
provenance_packaging.branding: Matches the bag's canvas line (Monogram, Damier Ebene, Damier Azur, etc.)
provenance_packaging.format_by_year: Introduced with the 2013 update; not present on 2007-2013 original-model bags.
provenance_packaging.authentication_notes: A current-era Neverfull missing its pochette is incomplete; conversely a pre-2013 bag legitimately may never have had one. Snippet-sourced.
provenance_packaging.value_impact_if_missing: Missing pochette typically reduces resale value/completeness for post-2013 bags.

price_history.notes: Approximate 2007 launch retail for the Monogram Neverfull MM (~$645) per resale/price-history aggregators (CloverSac). Snippet-sourced and approximate; date set to year-of-launch placeholder.

Approximate 2024/2025 US retail for the Monogram Neverfull MM (~$2,030) per multiple snippet sources (CloverSac, eBay 'Retail Price $2030', Bagaholic). Approximate; LV raises prices frequently. Date is an approximate placeholder, not a confirmed effective date.
